headline: Meta buys credits from four big US solar projects
summary: The agreement is the latest in a string of announcements Meta has made this year aimed at meeting the energy needs of its power-hungry data centers without harming the climate.  In its latest move, Meta signed four contracts with Chicago-based energy project developer Invenergy for 760 megawatts of solar electricity.  The projects will connect to the power grid between 2024 and 2027 and will be located in Ohio, Texas, New Mexico and Arkansas, Meta and Invenergy said in a joint statement.

headline: Meta shareholders seek sanctions for Sandberg, Zients for deleting Cambridge Analytica emails
summary: Attorneys for Meta shareholders asked a Delaware judge Monday to sanction the companys former Chief Operating Officer Sheryl Sandberg and fellow Facebook board member and current White House chief of staff Jeff Zients for deleting emails related to the Cambridge Analytica privacy scandal, despite being told to preserve such records.  The plaintiff attorneys contend that Sandberg and Zients used personal email accounts to communicate about key issues relating to their 2018 shareholder lawsuit that alleged Facebook officers and directors violated both the law and their fiduciary duties in failing for years to protect the privacy of user data.

headline: Mag 7 not critical for market success in 2025: Strategist
summary: While Bloomberg Intelligence is forecasting the Magnificent Sevens earnings growth — Meta Platforms (META), Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOGL, GOOG), Amazon (AMZN), Apple (AAPL), Nvidia (NVDA), Tesla (TSLA) — to drop from 2024s 34% to 18% in 2025, will the group of tech stocks be as pivotal for the S&P 500 (^GSPC) in the new year? Theyre obviously important for the market, but theyre not critical for the markets success. And we see signs that earnings growth is set to broaden out as we head into 2025, J.P. Morgan Private Bank US Head of Investment Strategy Jake Manoukian tells Seana Smith and Brad Smith on the Morning Brief. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Morning Brief here. ]This post was written by Luke Carberry Mogan.

headline: Meta adds live translation, AI video to Ray-Ban smart glasses
summary: The latest update adds video to Metas AI chatbot assistant, which allows the Ray-Ban smart glasses to process what the user is seeing and respond to questions in real-time.  The smart glasses will now be able to translate speech in real time between English and Spanish, French or Italian.  When youre talking to someone speaking one of those three languages, youll hear what they say in English through the glasses open-ear speakers or viewed as transcripts on your phone, and vice versa, Meta said in a blog.

headline: A TikTok Ban Would Be Great News for Meta Platforms, But It Also Highlights a Huge Risk
summary: In recent years, the biggest threat for Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) has been TikTok.  Metas response to that has been its Instagram Reels, which allows users to easily scroll through quick videos just as they would with TikTok.  Now, if TikTok ends up getting banned in the U.S., Meta could stand to benefit on multiple fronts: More users moving onto Instagram, and more ad dollars coming its way.
